failure_modes.text: Trend language without thresholds or owners creates slide theater rather than anticipatory readiness.
failure_modes.source_quote: horizon scanning becomes a slide ritual
failure_modes.mitigation: Attach thresholds, owners, and decision triggers to every regulatory signal: if a trend has no named owner and no action boundary, it is slide theater.

failure_modes.text: Headline overreaction mistakes signal detection for signal interpretation.
failure_modes.source_quote: confuses signal detection with signal interpretation
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate signal detection from signal interpretation: after identifying a regulatory change, run a structured impact assessment before reacting to the headline.

failure_modes.text: Confirmation-seeking can make the team read policy noise as support for a preferred roadmap.
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit the scan for confirmation bias: require the team to include at least one regulatory development that contradicts the preferred roadmap and assess its plausibility honestly.

premortem_questions.text: What weak signal would actually force a change in product, operating, or capital decisions if it strengthened?
premortem_questions.source_quote: weak signals can be turned into explicit scenarios

premortem_questions.text: What would we have to believe about timing, enforcement, or ownership for this policy scenario to matter now?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would you have to believe?

premortem_questions.text: If the worst regulatory case became the expected case, are we prepared enough today?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Is the worst case bad enough?

heuristics.text: Use a knowns-and-unknowns matrix to turn vague regulatory uncertainty into named monitoring work.
heuristics.source_quote: Known Knowns, Known Unknowns, Unknown Unknowns

heuristics.text: Write an active hypothesis about the future state and test it instead of collecting undirected trend slides.
Connect long-horizon patterns to present-day triggers, owners, and response options before urgency arrives.

curation_notes.summary: Chunk set stays close to the source's strongest failure modes, premortem questions, and repeatable heuristics.
donor_drops: Did not widen the chunk surface beyond clearly source-backed failure, premortem, and heuristic material.
open_questions: Some chunk text is compressed for operational reuse, but each item stays traceable to explicit source language.

allies: Relation-rich ally material was left for Wave 3 instead of being smuggled into chunk text.
antagonists: Bias and conflict material stronger as first-order relations was deferred to Wave 3.
structured_tensions: Any higher-order candidacy remains observational only in batch artifacts.
